name: Potato Soup Scented with Cumin
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Red skin potatoes, peeled and cut into 3/4-inch cubes
1 cup Half and half, or evaporated skim milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large, heavy, non-aluminum, deep-sided pan over medium-high heat.
2. When the butter is hot, add shallots and cook, stirring constantly, until soft, about 3 minutes.
3. Add potatoes and cumin to the pan; toss and cook for an additional minute.
4. Pour in the chicken stock. Bring to a boil, stirring frequently to prevent the potatoes from sticking to the pan.
5. Reduce heat and simmer until the potatoes are very tender, approximately 25 to 30 minutes.
6. Remove the soup from the heat and puree using a food processor, blender, or food mill.
7. Return the mixture to the pan and stir in the half and half (evaporated skim milk).
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste. The soup can be prepared up to 2 days ahead; cool, cover, and refrigerate.
9. To serve hot, reheat over medium heat while stirring until it is hot. To serve cold, taste the chilled soup and add salt and pepper if needed (cold foods often require extra seasoning).
10. Ladle into 8 bowls and garnish each with half a teaspoon of chopped cilantro. Makes 8 servings.
